data.title: In big win for farmers, EPA boosts biofuels quota to be blended into gas and diesel
data.description:
U.S. refiners must increase the amount of biofuels they blend into gasoline and diesel this year and next, after the Trump administration released long-awaited biofuels blending standards on Friday that favored farmers struggling with low crop prices and high input costs.

The new rule released by the Environmental Protection Agency increases biomass-based diesel—which is partly derived from soybeans—blending by more than 60%, and raises the biofuel requirement for all fuels by a lower percentage.

The rule requires refiners to mix a record 25.82B gallons of biofuels into conventional diesel and gasoline this year, nearly 8% higher than was advertised last June, the EPA said.

"Today's announcement, coupled with the Trump administration's E15 summertime waiver earlier this week, is a positive move for the nation's corn growers who are navigating an exceptionally difficult economic environment," the National ⁠Corn Growers Association said.

The refining industry criticized the move, with the American Fuel and Petrochemical Manufacturers calling it "baffling, with fuel prices already rising due to the conflict in Iran, the EPA is finalizing a rule that will make things far worse for consumers."

The EPA also said foreign fuels and feedstocks will receive only half of the RINs of U.S.-made products starting in 2028, a measure ​it said would help the domestic biofuel industry.

Potentially relevant stocks include Valero Energy (VLO), Marathon Petroleum (MPC), Phillips 66 (PSX), HF Sinclair (DINO), PBF Energy (PBF), Delek US (DK), Archer Daniels Midland (ADM), Bunge (BG), Green Plains (GPRE), Gevo (GEVO), Clean Energy Fuels (CLNE), REX American Resources (REX), Darling Ingredients (DAR), FutureFuel (FF).

ETFs: (SOYB), (CORN), (WEAT), (DBA), (MOO)

data.title: Troubadour Resources announces 10-for-1 share consolidation
data.description:
• Troubadour Resources (TR:CA) will implement a 10-for-1 share consolidation.
  • Outstanding shares will decrease from 70.07M to ~7.01M.

data.title: Femto to acquire 40% of Gilad
data.description:
• Femto Technologies (FMTOF) has entered into a share purchase agreement   to acquire 40% of the issued and outstanding shares of Gilad.
  • Pursuant to the acquisition, Femto will acquire from Gilad 43 previously unissued common shares of Gilad for $1M, to be paid in four equal quarterly installments of $250,000. 
  • The company will also acquire 14 Gilad shares from its subsidiaries in exchange for $250,000 and 69,811 subordinate voting shares in the capital of Femto at a deemed price of $0.589 per share. 
  • If Gilad achieves, within 24 months following the closing, revenues of at least $4.2M, Femto will make an additional payment of $1.68M.
  • Femto has the right, for 24 months after closing, to buy additional shares from the subsidiaries to increase its total stake in Gilad to 51%. It is also entitled to nominate one individual for election or appointment to Gilad’s board of directors following the closing and a second individual if the option is exercised.
  • Prior to the investment by Femto, Gilad holds cash of about $200,000.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: NextPlat approves 1-for-10 reverse stock split; shares down
data.description:
• NextPlat (NXPL) will implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split effective April 6, 2026, with split-adjusted trading beginning April 7, 2026.
  • The reverse split reduces outstanding shares from ~27.03M to 2.70M, with fractional shares cashed out.
  • The move aims to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price requirement and broaden investor interest.
  • NXPL shares down 31% post-market.

data.title: Main Street prices $200M public offering
data.description:
• Main Street Capital (MAIN) has priced an underwritten public offering of an additional $200M in aggregate principal amount of its 6.95% notes due 2029.
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be ~$204.1M.
  • Upon the issuance of the 2029 Notes, the outstanding aggregate principal amount of Main Street's 6.95% notes due 2029 will be $550.0 million. 
  • Main Street intends to initially use the net proceeds from the offering to repay outstanding indebtedness, to make investments in accordance with its investment objective and strategies, to make investments in marketable securities and idle funds investments, to pay operating expenses and other cash obligations, and for general corporate purposes.
  • MAIN +0.04% after hours to $51.55.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Reddit is the worst performing large-cap communication services stock YTD
data.description:
U.S. large-cap communication services stocks have fell in 2026, with the Communication Services Select Sector SPDR ETF down about 8.4% year-to-date through March.

Below is a list of the 10 worst-performing large-cap communication services stocks with market capitalizations of $10 billion or more. The list is arranged by their year-to-date performance, from the largest decline to the smallest.

The list is topped by Reddit, Inc. (RDDT), with a YTD performance down 44.64%. The Trade Desk, Inc. (TTD) and Paramount Skydance Corporation (PSKY) are next, with Roblox Corporation (RBLX) and Pinterest, Inc. (PINS) rounding out the rest of the top five.

Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (TTWO), Warner Music Group Corp. (WMG), and Fox Corporation (FOXA) also appear among the worst performers. Roku, Inc. (ROKU) and The Walt Disney Company (DIS) round out the bottom of the list with declines of 17.94% and 16.72%, respectively.

Here is the list:

  1. Reddit, Inc. (RDDT), YTD perf: -44.64%, Quant Rating: 3.05

  2. The Trade Desk, Inc. (TTD), YTD perf: -42.73%, Quant Rating: 2.05

  3. Paramount Skydance Corporation (PSKY), YTD perf: -33.73%, Quant Rating: 2.75

  4. Roblox Corporation (RBLX), YTD perf: -33.54%, Quant Rating: 1.91

  5. Pinterest, Inc. (PINS), YTD perf: -30.63%, Quant Rating: 2.11

  6. Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. (TTWO), YTD perf: -23.95%, Quant Rating: 3.28

  7. Warner Music Group Corp. (WMG), YTD perf: -21.03%, Quant Rating: 2.74

  8. Fox Corporation (FOXA), YTD perf: -19.75%, Quant Rating: 3.15

  9. Roku, Inc. (ROKU), YTD perf: -17.94%, Quant Rating: 3.33

  10. The Walt Disney Company (DIS), YTD perf: -16.72%, Quant Rating: 3.27

Communication Services ETFs: (XLC), (VOX), (IYZ), (RSPC), and (XTL)

data.title: InMed Pharma gets Nasdaq non-compliance notice
data.description:
• InMed Pharmaceuticals (INM) received a notice from Nasdaq for failing the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • The deficiency follows shares trading below $1.00 for 30 consecutive trading days (Feb 11–Mar 26, 2026).
  • The notice has no immediate impact, and shares will continue trading.
  • InMed has a 180-day period to regain compliance by achieving a $1.00+ closing price for 10 consecutive days.
  • The company may qualify for an additional 180-day extension if other listing criteria are met.
  • InMed may consider actions such as a reverse stock split to regain compliance.
  • Failure to meet requirements could lead to delisting, though the company has the right to appeal.

data.title: Gold falls for fourth straight week, faces added pressure from central bank reserve sales
data.description:
Gold futures turned higher Friday, as investors bought the dip after the metal tumbled in the previous session on a stronger dollar and Treasury yields, but the rebound was not enough to prevent a fourth consecutive weekly loss, marking its longest weekly losing streak since 2023.

Despite Friday's gain, bearish pressures remain for gold as doubts persist over any potential for a ceasefire in the Middle East war, even after President Trump pledged to refrain from attacks on Iran's energy sites for an additional 10 days.

Gold prices have fallen nearly 15% since the war began on February 28, trading like a risk asset rather than a safe haven, moving largely in tandem with stocks and in an inverse relationship with oil.

"The recent price action highlights how gold, during a supply-driven macro shock, has shifted into a source of liquidity... behaving more like a risk asset and moving in line with broader market stress, thereby failing to provide traditional safe-haven support," Saxo Bank analysts said in a note.

Gold is facing extra pressure from central bank reserve sales, Julius Baer analyst Carsten Menke said in a note, even as his long-term view stays constructive.

Menke said Turkey has sold ~54 metric tons of gold since the start of the Iran war to support the lira, and Poland is also considering a sale, adding that involuntary selling is more concerning than voluntary rebalancing, as they lack an element of control.

The war has caused "pretty significant economic damage for Middle Eastern nations, who have been some of the participants in the official sector gold purchases," TD Securities analyst Daniel Ghali wrote. "While Turkey grabbed the headlines, this is probably a more prevalent trend across energy-import nations."

The economic shock from the war likely will hurt demand for bullion from some central banks while forcing others to sell from gold reserves to meet dollar-denominated obligations, Ghali said.

On Friday, front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for March delivery jumped 2.6% to $4,492.00/oz, and front-month Comex March silver (XAGUSD:CUR) climbed 2.8% to $69.545/oz; for the full week, gold slipped 1.7% for its fourth straight weekly loss, but silver edged 0.3% higher, snapping a three-week negative streak.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Jericho Energy announces COO appointment
data.description:
• Jericho Energy Ventures (JEV:CA) promoted Jake Royster to chief operating officer from his prior role as director of operations.
  • Royster's achievements include lowest per-barrel lifting costs in over eight quarters and maintaining profitability despite a 17% price decline.
  • He also improved operational efficiency, communication processes, and safety standards across field operations.

data.title: Vertical Aerospace shares set record low, reiterates "going concern” warning -- update
data.description:
Updated 3/27 17:00 ET with a statement from a Vertical Aerospace spokesperson regarding the company's financial performance and capital position.

Shares of Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) are under pressure on Tuesday and dipped to a record low after the company reiterated that it has “substantial doubt it can continue to conduct its normal business operations in the foreseeable future” without liquidating assets or restructuring its obligations. This is a statement the company has repeated since 2023. 

“Our limited cash and cash equivalents, recurring losses from operations, and dependency on raising additional capital indicate that a material uncertainty exists that may cast significant doubt regarding our ability to continue as a going concern,” the company said in a  filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

For 2025, Vertical Aerospace (EVTL) reported a net profit of £202.6M ($271.6M) for 2025, or £2.63 per share, but a loss of £0.57 per share ($0.76) on a diluted basis, missing expectations for a loss of £0.0075 ($0.01). Last year, Vertical (EVTL) reported a loss of £38.46 per diluted share.

On its balance sheet, Vertical (EVTL) raised $175M in capital, ended the year with $93M in cash and cash equivalents, and anticipates approximately $23M in near-term receipts from R&D tax reliefs and another $5M in government grants and VAT. At the same time, Vertical (EVTL) expects to spend $195M in flight testing, certification, and manufacturing, an outflow that must be covered through either government grants, R&D tax relief, or additional funding. 

Operationally, Vertical (EVTL) is making solid progress towards its goal of achieving certification and scaling production of its electric vertical take-off and landing ((eVTOL)) aircraft. This includes the launch of Valo, its commercial eVTOL aircraft designed for 4-6 passengers, its battery pilot program line, and completed a third prototype to provide increased flight capacity.

It also completed hover, vertical flight, and wingborne flight, including the first winged eVTOL flight in open European airspace and airport-to-airport flight at the Royal International Air Tattoo.

However, despite operational achievements, Vertical (EVTL) cautioned that since the market for its offerings is still in the relatively early stage of growth, if these markets do not continue to grow, grow more slowly than expected, or fail to grow as large as expected, the company’s business, financial condition, and results of operations could be harmed.

“If we are not able to overcome these barriers, our business, prospects, operating results, and financial condition will be negatively impacted, and our ability to grow our business will be harmed,” Vertical said in its SEC filing.

Nonetheless, Vertical (EVTL) CEO Stuart Simpson remains optimistic as the company is advancing its plans to expand at Cotswold Airport. 

“We’ve made strong progress across the business—achieving key piloted flight milestones, advancing transition testing, and launching Valo to the market. The response from customers and partners has been very encouraging, with renewed order activity and growing confidence in our program. We are now firmly focused on certification and scaling production.”

Statement from Vertical Aerospace spokesperson: As disclosed in the Annual Report and discussed on the Q4 earnings call, we’ve made strong progress across our flight test program, manufacturing, and commercial development over the past twelve months and are closer than ever to certification and entry into service. Vertical’s anticipated short-term liquidity totals approximately $85 million.

The language cited in our Annual Report is a standard accounting disclosure for a company at Vertical’s stage of development and has appeared consistently in the company’s filings since 2023. It does not reflect any new development.
